summary: solana-key-utils decrypts and executes bundled payload at import with cron persistence
details: On module load, index.js schedules a delayed routine that AES-256-CBC-decrypts test/fixtures/keypairs.dat (a ~40KB opaque ciphertext shipped as a fake test fixture) using a hardcoded key and IV, writes the decrypted JavaScript to ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js with mode 0o700, installs a crontab entry running `/usr/bin/node ~/.cache-db/.node-sync/syncd.js` every 12 hours, and spawns the staged file detached. The hidden dot-path staging directory, obfuscated variable names, ~42-second delay, and cover-story filenames ("keypairs.dat", "syncd.js") indicate deliberate concealment. Persistence via user crontab survives removal of the package. The package's declared purpose ("solana key utilities") is unrelated to any legitimate need for decrypting an embedded blob or installing a scheduled task.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
